_meta.description: AstroPay image library — extracted from Figma Images page. Source of truth for ALL imagery used by paid media, email, and in-app creatives.
rules: ABSOLUTE RULE — every image used by any agent MUST come from one of the six sections on this page: Traveler, Places, Freelancer, Digital nomad, Product Mockup, Product. No external URLs. No free-floating canvas frames. No images from any other Figma file or page.

DEFAULT POOL — for any standard paid media creative, pick from the four lifestyle categories ONLY: Traveler, Places, Freelancer, Digital nomad. These cover ~95% of briefs (PIX, Infinite, Freelance SMB, brand awareness, retention, lifecycle, social, etc.).

EXCHANGE GATE — Product and Product Mockup are OPT-IN ONLY. Use them ONLY when the brief explicitly mentions currency exchange, FX, conversion, multicurrency, or names a currency pair (e.g., BRL→USD, ARS→EUR). If the brief does not mention any of these, do NOT pick from Product or Product Mockup, even if they technically fit the format. When in doubt, default to a lifestyle category and ask the user before reaching for Product/Product Mockup.
Never use findAll() on this page (200+ nodes = timeout) — always findOne() by exact name.
Image must match the campaign category — see selection_rules below.
Cache the imageHash immediately after retrieval — never re-fetch in the same task.
_meta.sync_note: Auto-detected change on 2026-05-01 — review and update manually if needed

traveler.description: Lifestyle travel imagery — airports, beaches, global destinations, aerial shorelines. Aspirational, on-the-go.

places.description: Destinations, cities, venues, lifestyle locations. Premium, destination-driven. Coffee shops, hotel exteriors, urban scenes.

selection_examples.name: coffe 03 - El cafe de la esquina 1

freelancer.description: Freelancers, entrepreneurs, SMB owners working independently. Laptop, home office, never generic fintech stock.

digital_nomad.description: Remote workers, laptops in cafes, professionals on the go. Warm, relatable, urban — bridges traveler and freelancer.

product.description: EXCHANGE-ONLY. Flat 1:1 product images of the AstroPay exchange flow per language pair. Six images covering BRL→USD/EUR/ARS in PT-BR and ARS→USD/EUR/BRL in ES.
product._use_rule: Opt-in only. Use ONLY when the brief explicitly mentions currency exchange, FX, conversion, multicurrency, or names a currency pair. Never use as a generic product shot for non-exchange briefs (PIX, Infinite GTM, brand awareness, Freelance SMB, lifecycle, etc.).
product._use_when_triggered: Once the exchange gate is open, use Product (flat) when the brief asks for a clean exchange screen as content with no phone silhouette (e.g., 1:1 design_2 in-feed retargeting, in-app illustration headers).

product_mockup.description: EXCHANGE-ONLY. Phone-mockup previews of the AstroPay exchange flow. Same six currency pairs as Product, with the phone-frame artifact baked in.
product_mockup._use_rule: Opt-in only. Use ONLY when the brief explicitly mentions currency exchange, FX, conversion, multicurrency, or names a currency pair. Never use as generic mockup imagery for non-exchange briefs.
product_mockup._use_when_triggered: Once the exchange gate is open, use Product Mockup for hero / fullbg layouts (design_4, design_5) where the phone-shaped silhouette adds context. For clean in-feed flat images, use Product instead.

selection_rules._default_pool: Unless the brief explicitly asks for an exchange / FX / multicurrency creative, pick from these four lifestyle categories ONLY: traveler, places, freelancer, digital_nomad. Product and Product Mockup are OFF-LIMITS by default.
selection_rules.BR campaigns: digital_nomad or freelancer — warm, relatable, urban
selection_rules.Freelance SMB: freelancer — specific, not generic fintech stock
selection_rules.PIX (AR → BR): traveler imagery is correct — campaign IS about traveling to Brazil
selection_rules.Brand awareness / lifestyle / retention: pick from any of the four lifestyle categories that matches market and product. Never reach for Product or Product Mockup just because the format is square or vertical.
selection_rules._exchange_gate: The two rules below ONLY apply when the brief mentions currency exchange, FX, conversion, multicurrency, or names a currency pair (BRL→USD, ARS→EUR, etc.). If none of those triggers are present, ignore the exchange rules and use the lifestyle pool above.
selection_rules.Exchange (retargeting, in-feed): ONLY when triggered by the gate above. First choice: digital_nomad or freelancer for lifestyle context. Use product (flat) only if the brief specifically asks for the UI to be the hero. NEVER travel-themed (Paris cafe, airport, suitcase) — that suggests a trip campaign.
selection_rules.Exchange (awareness / hero / fullbg): ONLY when triggered by the gate above. Use product_mockup matched to the correct currency pair (brl_to_usd, ars_to_eur, etc.) and language variant (_PTBR for BR, _ES for AR).

currency_pair_routing._note: USE ONLY when the brief explicitly mentions currency exchange, FX, conversion, or names a currency pair. Do not consult this map for non-exchange briefs.
